semanticCore: Ant, active walk, slander, gossip, or tiny spots
quranicRelevance: Valley of ants (Naml - 'wa di an-namli qalat namlatun ya ayyuha an-namlu').

keyDerivations.meaning: Ants / an ant (tiny organized social insect)

figurativeOrRareUsages: قَالَتْ نَمْلَةٌ يَا أَيُّهَا النَّمْلُ ادْخُلُوا مَسَاكِنَكُمْ لَا يَحْطِمَنَّكُمْ سُلَيْمَانُ (27:18): An ant said: 'O ants, enter your dwellings lest Solomon and his hosts crush you' (animal intelligence and warning speech).
summaryEn: The root refers to ants (Naml), detailing Solomon's encounters with the organized ant valley and his laughter at the insect's protective warning call.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى النمل الصغير (نمل)، واصفاً مرور جيش سليمان بوادي النمل وتنبيه نملة ذكية لبقية النمل بالاحتماء لئلا يُحطمن.
rawText: أَنْمَلَةٌ (thus generally written, Msb) The head [or end] of a finger; (S, Msb;) i. e. (Msb) the joint (مَفْصِل) (Az, Msb) in which is the nail: (Az, Msb, K:) [i. e. the ungual phalanx, with, or without, the flesh upon it: (see قَصَبٌ:)] or a joint, or an articulation, (عُقْدَة) of the fingers. (Msb.) تَنَمُّلٌ 5 A formication or stinging, as of the torpedo fish: see “ Abdollatiphi Hist. Aeg. Comp., ” p. 82. نَمَّلَ الثَّوْبَ 2 [He mended a garment] i. q. رَفَأَهُ. (TA in art. لقط.) نَمْلُ سُلَيْمَانَ Red ants: see أَحْوَى, in art. حو.
